name: pressure_fed_lox_kerosene_system_8ba12874
description: Design a pressure-fed rocket engine feed system using LOX (liquid oxygen) and kerosene as propellants, with nitrogen (GN2) as the pressurant gas. The system must include: GN2 tank for pressurization, LOX tank, kerosene tank, a rocket engine block, pressurization lines from GN2 to each propellant tank, and feed lines from each tank to the engine. FIXED quantities: component architecture (GN2 tank, LOX tank, kerosene tank, engine, pressurization lines, feed lines). TUNABLE quantities: tank pressures, volumes, line dimensions, flow rates, and timing. The system must deliver both propellants to the engine with non-zero mass flow through each feed line.

notes: Use GN2 to pressurize both LOX and kerosene tanks
Ensure both propellants flow to the engine
Standard pressure-fed architecture with separate feed lines
Preserve the Engine node and the two feed Series connections into it.
Tune feed CdA values before changing topology.
If physical warnings mention a component, fix that component first.

checks.description: Simulation must complete without crashing or invalid configuration
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
LOX feed line must deliver propellant to engine (non-zero mass flow)
Kerosene feed line must deliver propellant to engine (non-zero mass flow)
LOX feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
Kerosene feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
